personas: Primary: Budget-conscious millennial (25-35) who shops on mobile during commute
Primary: Busy professional (35-45) who prefers desktop, values efficiency
Secondary: First-time online shopper (55+) who needs reassurance
journeyMaps: Awareness → Product Discovery → Add to Cart → Checkout → Confirmation
Cart abandonment → Return via email → Complete purchase
usabilityFindings: Average checkout completion: 68%
Guest checkout users complete 23% faster
Mobile users abandon at shipping step 41% more than desktop
painPoints: Unexpected shipping costs at final step

sitemap: Home > Cart > Guest Checkout > Shipping > Payment > Review > Confirmation
Home > Account > Saved Addresses > Add New
informationArchitecture.navigationSystem: Linear checkout funnel with persistent cart summary sidebar
userFlows: Guest flow: Cart → Email → Shipping → Payment → Review → Done
Returning user: Cart → Login → Shipping (prefilled) → Payment → Done
Error flow: Invalid card → Inline error → Retry → Success

wireframes.name: Cart Page
wireframes.description: Shopping cart with item list and summary

visualEffects: Smooth transitions on form focus (200ms ease)

designSystem.name: CommerceKit
guidelines: Primary buttons for main actions only
Form labels always visible above inputs
Error messages inline, never in alerts

componentLibrary.name: CartItem
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Quantity changes announced to screen readers. Remove button has aria-label.

componentLibrary.name: CheckoutStepper
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Uses aria-current='step' for current step

interactionPatterns.behavior: Show dropdown of suggestions after 3 characters
interactionPatterns.accessibility: Announce number of suggestions found. Arrow keys navigate list.

interactionPatterns.behavior: Show inline error below field if empty or invalid

adaptiveStrategies: Single column on mobile with stacked elements
Sidebar becomes bottom sheet on mobile
priorityContent: Cart summary always visible on desktop
Order total sticky on mobile scroll

personas: Primary: Data analyst (28-40) who lives in dashboards, needs customization
Primary: Manager (35-50) who checks metrics weekly, wants quick insights
Secondary: Executive (45-60) who views on mobile, needs high-level KPIs
journeyMaps: Login → Dashboard Overview → Drill into Metric → Export Report
Morning ritual: Check key metrics → Investigate anomalies → Take action
usabilityFindings: Users spend 68% of time on first 3 widgets
Dark mode preference: 62% of users
Customization increases daily active usage by 34%
painPoints: Overwhelming default view with too many metrics
No way to share specific views with team

informationArchitecture.navigationSystem: Top navigation with dashboard tabs, left sidebar for widget list
userFlows: First login: Onboarding wizard → Default dashboard → Customize
Return user: Quick access to last 3 views
Widget flow: Add → Configure → Resize → Arrange → Save

designSystem.name: DashboardUI
guidelines: Widgets can be dragged but snapping to grid
Charts use consistent color encoding across dashboard

componentLibrary.name: MetricCard
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Change values announced with aria-live region. Trend uses icon + text, not color alone.

componentLibrary.name: ChartWidget
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Provides data table alternative via toggle. Keyboard navigable legend.

interactionPatterns.behavior: Widget becomes semi-transparent, shows drop zones
interactionPatterns.accessibility: Keyboard alternative: Tab to widget, Enter to move, Arrow keys to reposition
Each option announces date range clearly

accessibilityCompliance.focusIndicators: Dashed outline for focus, solid for active drag

priorityContent: Charts simplify to key data on mobile

personas: Primary: Small business owner (30-50) with limited tech skills, needs quick wins
Secondary: Startup founder (25-35) who wants to explore deeply
Tertiary: Enterprise admin (40-55) who delegates to team

journeyMaps: Signup → Email Verify → Role Selection → Workspace Setup → Import Data → Feature Tour → Dashboard
First login → Skip tour → Never returns → High churn risk
usabilityFindings: Completion rate with 3 steps: 89%
Completion rate with 7 steps: 34%
Users who complete setup have 3x higher retention

sitemap: Welcome > Role > Workspace > Import > Tour > Dashboard
informationArchitecture.navigationSystem: Step-by-step progress bar, each step independent
userFlows: Quick setup: Role → Workspace → Done (2 min)
Full setup: All steps including import and tour (15 min)

visualEffects: Role cards have hover scale (1.02) and shadow lift

componentLibrary.name: ProgressStepper
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Uses aria-label for each step. Current step announced on focus.

componentLibrary.name: RoleCard
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Selected state announced. Icon has aria-hidden if decorative.

interactionPatterns.trigger: Field blur or 2 second idle after typing
interactionPatterns.behavior: Save to server, show subtle success toast

accessibilityCompliance.focusIndicators: Focus ring on all interactive elements

priorityContent: Primary CTA always visible above fold

personas: Primary: Privacy-conscious user (25-45) who wants granular control
Secondary: Enterprise admin who manages team settings
journeyMaps: Profile > Security > Notifications > Billing > Team > API > Appearance
usabilityFindings: Most visited: Security and Notifications
Users expect settings to persist immediately
Nested settings cause confusion

sitemap: Profile / Security / Notifications / Billing / Team / API / Appearance
userFlows: Quick toggle: Find setting → Toggle → See confirmation
Deep config: Tab → Section → Expand → Configure → Save

componentLibrary.name: ToggleSetting
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Uses checkbox role. State announced on toggle.

componentLibrary.name: SettingSection
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Section toggle button has aria-expanded.

personas: Primary: Content consumer (18-35) who scrolls for entertainment
Secondary: Content creator (20-40) who posts and engages
Tertiary: Silent observer who never posts

journeyMaps: Open app → Scroll feed → Like/Comment → Share → Close
Open app → Check notifications → Post content → Monitor engagement
usabilityFindings: Users average 2.3 minutes per session
Pull-to-refresh used 4x per session on average
Infinite scroll increases session length by 47%

informationArchitecture.navigationSystem: Bottom tab bar (Feed, Search, Create, Notifications, Profile)
userFlows: Consume: Scroll → Tap → View detail → Back to feed
Create: Tab Create → Add Media → Add Caption → Post → Share options

componentLibrary.name: FeedPost
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Alt text required for all images. Actions have aria-labels.

componentLibrary.name: EngagementButton
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Active state uses aria-pressed. Count announced on focus.

adaptiveStrategies: Tab bar adapts to gesture bar on newer phones

personas: Primary: Chronic patient (45-65) who manages ongoing care
Primary: Parent managing children's health (30-45)
Secondary: Caregiver helping elderly relative (35-55)

journeyMaps: Login → Dashboard → Upcoming appointments → Book new → Select provider → Confirm
Login → Messages → New message → Select recipient → Type → Send → Confirmation
usabilityFindings: Users over 50 prefer larger text and clear CTAs
Mobile appointments booked 3x more than desktop
Secure messaging reduces phone call volume by 60%
painPoints: Navigation too complex for elderly users

sitemap: Dashboard > Appointments > Messages > Health Records > Billing > Profile
informationArchitecture.navigationSystem: Large icon + label tabs, high contrast
userFlows: Patient: Dashboard → Schedule → Provider → Time → Confirm
Caregiver: Switch profile → View records → Message doctor

designSystem.name: MediCare UI
principles: Trust and security visible in every interaction
High contrast and large touch targets

componentLibrary.name: AppointmentCard
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Date announced by screen reader. Cancel requires confirmation dialog.

componentLibrary.name: SecureMessage
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Uses role='region' with aria-label for message thread.

adaptiveStrategies: Text scales up on larger screens

personas: Primary: Family planner (30-50) who books trips for 3-6 people
Primary: Business traveler (25-45) who needs efficiency
Secondary: Adventure traveler (20-35) who seeks unique experiences

journeyMaps: Search → Results → Filters → Select → Details → Travelers → Payment → Confirmation
Search → Results → Map view → Select → Details → Book
usabilityFindings: Users abandon at payment step 28% due to hidden fees
Price comparison increases conversion 45%
Map view increases engagement 67% on mobile

sitemap: Search > Results > Details > Travelers > Payment > Confirmation > Itinerary
informationArchitecture.navigationSystem: Linear booking flow with persistent price summary
userFlows: Quick book: Search → Results → Select → Travelers → Payment → Done
Comparison: Results → Compare → Select → Book

visualEffects: Card flip animation for deal highlights

componentLibrary.name: ResultCard
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Price announced. Availability state announced on focus.

componentLibrary.name: PriceSummary
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Total prominently announced. Breakdown available via expandable section.

adaptiveStrategies: Filters in bottom sheet on mobile
priorityContent: Price always visible in sticky header

personas: Primary: Daily banking user (25-50) who checks transactions often
Primary: Small business owner (30-55) managing cash flow
Secondary: Investor tracking portfolio

journeyMaps: Home → Transactions → Transaction Detail → Categorize/Tag → Export
Home → Send Money → Select Recipient → Enter Amount → Confirm → Receipt
usabilityFindings: Fingerprint/face login used by 78% of users
Instant transfers increase user satisfaction 65%
Transaction search is top-3 used feature
painPoints: Slow bank sync causing pending transactions

sitemap: Send > Recipient > Amount > Review > Confirm > Receipt
userFlows: Quick pay: Home → Pay → Recent → Amount → Confirm
Review: Transactions → Filter → Search → Detail → Categorize

visualEffects: Amount counter animation on balance update

designSystem.name: BankKit
principles: Money movements are celebratory or cautionary

componentLibrary.name: TransactionRow
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Amount announced with color. Pending state announced.

componentLibrary.name: AmountInput
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Currency announced. Large, clear numerals. Error announced immediately.

personas: Primary: Self-motivated learner (20-40) completing certifications
Primary: Student (18-25) taking university courses
Secondary: Corporate trainee (25-50) in required training

journeyMaps: Dashboard > Course > Module > Lesson > Quiz > Certificate
Dashboard > Progress > Review > Continue where left off
usabilityFindings: Completion drops 40% after first week without interaction
Video captions increase completion 25%
Progress visualization increases motivation
painPoints: Video player lacks playback speed control

informationArchitecture.navigationSystem: Sidebar with course outline, main content area
userFlows: Continue: Dashboard → Resume → Last position → Continue
New course: Dashboard → Enroll → Intro → First lesson → Quiz

componentLibrary.name: VideoPlayer
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Full keyboard controls. Captions toggle. Transcript available as scrollable text.

componentLibrary.name: LessonCard
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Completion announced. Locked state explained.

personas: Primary: Remote team member (25-45) collaborating daily
Primary: Project manager (30-50) coordinating across time zones
Secondary: Client collaborator (25-55) with limited access

journeyMaps: Dashboard > Document > Edit > Comment > Resolve > Share
Notification > Document > View > Suggest > Author Reviews > Accept
usabilityFindings: Real-time presence increases collaboration confidence 58%
Comment threads reduce email by 40%
Version history accessed by 23% of users monthly
painPoints: Confusion about who can edit what

informationArchitecture.navigationSystem: Sidebar with document tree, top bar with actions
userFlows: Edit: Open doc → Real-time edit → Auto-save → Version created
Collaborate: Comment → Tag user → Notification → Resolve → Done

designSystem.name: CollabKit
principles: Awareness of others is calming and productive
guidelines: Comments can be resolved or reopened, never deleted

componentLibrary.name: PresenceAvatar
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: User name announced. Idle state announced after 2 min.

componentLibrary.name: CommentThread
componentLibrary.accessibilityNotes: Thread linked to document location. Resolved state announced.

interactionPatterns.behavior: Show colored cursor with name label
interactionPatterns.accessibility: Toggle to show only active user cursors or none

interactionPatterns.behavior: Show 'Saving...' then 'All changes saved' with timestamp

accessibilityCompliance.focusIndicators: Focus ring on toolbar, focus mode hides comments
